actions.name: addCustomer
actions.repoCall: repo.addCustomer({required String name, required String phone, String? note, String? address})

payload.name: id
payload.note: NOT sent by the as-built repo call (drift uses int autoIncrement). Per conventions S-2/§IDs the client mints a UUIDv7 id on every create; id is a required string on the create payload and the create is idempotent on a re-seen id. Add on the wire.

payload.name: name
payload.note: _name.text.trim(); UI requires non-empty (trimmed) to enable Save.

payload.name: phone
payload.note: _phoneDigits — digits-only string, UI-validated to exactly 11 digits before Save enables. Drift column defaults to '' but the screen always sends a value.

payload.name: note
payload.note: _notes.text.trim(); sent as null when empty (nullable). Maps to Customers.note (drift column named 'note', not 'notes').

notSentByClientButServerOwned.name: createdAt
notSentByClientButServerOwned.note: As-built set client-side (DateTime.now()); per conventions offline-first protocol updatedAt is server-authoritative. createdAt present on drift Customers table.

notSentByClientButServerOwned.name: updatedAt
notSentByClientButServerOwned.note: Server-authoritative timestamp per sync protocol; Customers already carries updatedAt as-built.

notSentByClientButServerOwned.name: version
notSentByClientButServerOwned.note: Monotonic per-row version added by schema agent for offline sync (If-Match writes); not present as-built.

notSentByClientButServerOwned.name: businessId
notSentByClientButServerOwned.note: Tenant scope — injected from JWT claim, never sent by client.

notSentByClientButServerOwned.name: syncState
notSentByClientButServerOwned.note: Client-local sync bookkeeping only (marked pending on insert); not a wire/backend field.

expectedResponse.note: As-built repo.addCustomer returns the local int row id; on the wire the create returns the created Customer resource (idempotent: re-seen id returns existing, 200).

authContext.note: Creating customers is core recovery, not gated by plan. Per conventions roles model, staff can read/write customers (owner also). Both owner and staff may add a customer; not owner-only.

deviceContactImport.description: Local device-only feature: opens the OS contact book in a styled sheet and returns a PickedContact ({name, phone}). NOT a backend call — no API contract. Degrades politely on web/denied-permission/emulator.
deviceContactImport.note: On pick, fills _name (picked.name) and _phone (formatPhone(picked.phone)); phone digits normalised to \D-stripped. address and note are never populated by contact import.

uiStatesHandled: validation: name must be non-empty (trimmed) and phone must be exactly 11 digits; Save button disabled until both pass (_canSave)
validation-error: inline phone error 'Enter a valid phone number' shown after the phone field is touched when non-empty digits are not 11 long (_phoneError)
success: on save the screen pops and shows a snackbar '<name> added — record their first debt?' with a 'Record' action deep-linking to /record-debt?customer=<id>
no-explicit-error-state: _save() has no try/catch — offline-first write is assumed to complete locally and instantly; no loading spinner or failure UI as-built
